# task\-audit\-mutation\-recovery — Make multi\-file mutations crash\-recoverable
status: ready  priority: 1  scope: scope-core

Close the remaining high\-priority crash\-consistency gap from audit H4 before adding more lifecycle mutations\. Design and implement a recoverable protocol for canonical operations that update multiple records and events so interruption at any write boundary is detected and deterministically completed or rolled back before later mutations proceed\.

## Acceptance
- An ADR selects a durable journal/replay or staged\-manifest protocol and documents failure points, recovery ownership, and event ordering\.
- A shared core mutation primitive records durable intent under the ledger lock, applies record/event writes, and clears the intent only after the operation is durable\.
- Recovery runs under the same ledger lock, is idempotent, and occurs before a new canonical mutation can proceed\.
- Claim, release, finish, issue creation, handoff creation, and message posting use the recoverable primitive or have a documented proof that a single write is sufficient\.
- Crash\-injection tests interrupt each multi\-write operation after every durable boundary and prove recovery leaves coherent records, claims, events, and timestamps\.
- Validation reports unrecoverable or malformed mutation intent with coded diagnostics and no raw stack leakage\.
